fields.description:
Split src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py (907 LOC, over the 800 soft cap) by extracting the code-review post-verdict finalization cluster into a new strict-leaf module src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py (the code_review/ subpackage already exists; no new __init__.py). This is exactly the seam the docs/architecture.md offenders row for gate_dispatch names as its 'Next seam' — that remedy row is current (not stale) and is deleted by this ticket.

Move (locate by NAME): _resolve_or_create_session_artifact, _link_session_artifact, STEP_ASSEMBLE_DIFF, _HIGH_PRIORITY_FLOOR, _count_diff_lines, _attach_code_review_metrics, and factor the success-branch body of _run_code_review_gate into a new finalize_code_review_verdict(verdict, *, request, prep, cfg, runner_sel, total_ms) -> dict in the leaf — leaving _run_code_review_gate a thin sequencer that runs the workflow and, on success, lazily imports finalize and delegates (matching the file's all-lazy cross-module import style).

STRICT LEAF: finalize.py imports nothing from gate_dispatch. gate_dispatch's shared step-id constants are the PUBLIC names STEP_VERIFY ('verify') and STEP_DECIDE ('decide') (lines 56-57) plus the private _LLM_STEP_KINDS (line 217). To avoid a back-import, finalize.py re-declares these as small local constants (_STEP_VERIFY = 'verify', _STEP_DECIDE = 'decide', _LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({'agent','batch'})) with a mirror comment naming the gate_dispatch originals. The moved _attach_code_review_metrics body currently references STEP_VERIFY/STEP_DECIDE by their public names; those two references are updated to the local private names — a mechanical rename incident to the move (identical string values), NOT a logic change.

Back-compat: gate_dispatch re-exports _attach_code_review_metrics (top-level import from finalize, # noqa: F401) — load-bearing: tests/unit/test_code_review_fp_ledger.py calls gate_dispatch._attach_code_review_metrics at 7 sites. The top-level re-export is verified cycle-free (code_review/__init__ imports only contracts + shim; shim imports gate_dispatch lazily; config.py never imports gate_dispatch).

This is a pure/mechanical relocation — no signature or logic changes beyond the constant-name rename above; the exact ordering and best-effort try/except guards in the moved success block are preserved (session tests assert an artifact/emit failure never fails the review). E5 testing-plan is not-applicable — the existing test suites are the oracle.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] NEW src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py exists and is in [100,800] LOC (wc -l).
- [ ] src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py is <=800 LOC (wc -l).
- [ ] finalize_code_review_verdict is importable: python -c 'from rebar.llm.code_review.finalize import finalize_code_review_verdict'.
- [ ] finalize.py contains no import of gate_dispatch: grep -R gate_dispatch src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py is empty.
- [ ] gate_dispatch.py removed from .github/module-size-allowlist.txt AND .github/module-size-ceilings.txt, and its remedy row deleted from the docs/architecture.md offenders table.
- [ ] _attach_code_review_metrics is re-exported from gate_dispatch (back-compat): gate_dispatch._attach_code_review_metrics still resolves.
- [ ] tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py passes (allowlist == computed over-cap set == ceilings keys).
- [ ] tests/unit/test_code_review_fp_ledger.py, test_code_review_gate_session.py, test_code_review_ws4.py, test_gate_engine_cutover.py all pass.
- [ ] make lint reports All checks passed!
